0.q: The salt overly sensitive (SOS) signalling pathway is remarkable in the transport of toxic ions. SOS1 encodes for a plasma membrane antiporter Na+/H+ which removes Na+ from cells.
The expression levels of CmHKT1 and CmSOS1 increased in the CmMYB121-SRDX lines, followed by those in WT, and decreased in the CmMYB121-OE lines.

1.q: DgWRKY5 could regulate the expression of ROS scavenging enzymes, enhance cell membrane protection system and weaken the membrane lipid peroxidation to improve the salt tolerance of plants

2.q: The gene expression levels of oxidoreductase increased in CmDOF18-overexpressing transgenic plants but decreased in CmDOF18-SRDX gene-silenced transgenic plants.

4.q: CmHSFA4 overexpression reduced ROS levels and activated ROS scavenger activities… SOD, APX, CAT in OX plants were higher compared with WT at day 0 and day 7 of the salinity stress treatment.

5.q: qRT-PCR analyses confirmed that stable expression of CmDOF2 in Arabidopsis led to increased transcript levels of key salt-responsive genes, including components of the SOS signaling pathway (AtSOS1, AtSOS2, AtSOS3).

7.q: When the gene was constitutively expressed in Arabidopsis thaliana, it increased plant sensitivity to ABA and reduced stomatal aperture.

8.q: CmMYB121 overexpression increased H2O2 and O2− accumulation in chrysanthemum; activity levels of antioxidant enzymes CAT, SOD, and APX were the highest in the SRDX lines, followed by WT and OE lines.

10.q: Protein–protein interaction assays identified an interaction between CmHSFA4 and CmMYBS3, a transcriptional repressor, and recruited the corepressor TOPLESS (CmTPL) to inhibit CmMYB121 transcription.

11.q: The CmHSFA4–CmMYBS3 complex recruits CmTPL via the EAR motif at the CmMYBS3 C-terminus to impair the acetylation level of CmMYB121 and inhibit its transcription.

12.q: Unigense68526-All was suppressed by CmHSFA4 under CK treatment [Log2 (CK–H4/CK–WT) = −1.53]

13.q: After salt stress, relative expression level of DgAPX and DgCAT genes which encoded ROS scavenging enzymes were significantly (P < 0.05) up-regulated in DgWRKY5 transgenic lines, comparing with WT plants increased by about two and three times, respectively.

14.q: After salt stress, relative expression level of DgAPX and DgCAT genes which encoded ROS scavenging enzymes were significantly (P < 0.05) up-regulated in DgWRKY5 transgenic lines, comparing with WT plants increased by about two and three times, respectively.

15.q: The expression of ABA-responsive genes DgNCED3A, DgNCED3B and DgCuZnSOD had also greatly raised in transgenic lines compared to WT under salt treatment.

16.q: The expression of ABA-responsive genes DgNCED3A, DgNCED3B and DgCuZnSOD had also greatly raised in transgenic lines compared to WT under salt treatment.

17.q: The expression of ABA-responsive genes DgNCED3A, DgNCED3B and DgCuZnSOD had also greatly raised in transgenic lines compared to WT under salt treatment.

18.q: Other abiotic stress-response genes, such as DgP5CS, which functions in osmotic adjustment, DgCSD1, and DgCSD2 were all significantly (P < 0.05) increased in transgenic plants than WT under salinity condition.

19.q: Other abiotic stress-response genes, such as DgP5CS, which functions in osmotic adjustment, DgCSD1, and DgCSD2 were all significantly (P < 0.05) increased in transgenic plants than WT under salinity condition.

20.q: Other abiotic stress-response genes, such as DgP5CS, which functions in osmotic adjustment, DgCSD1, and DgCSD2 were all significantly (P < 0.05) increased in transgenic plants than WT under salinity condition.

21.q: the transcript levels of ion homeostasis genes CmSOS1 and CmHKT2 in CmHSFA4 were much higher than the WT with or without salinity

22.q: the transcript levels of ion homeostasis genes CmSOS1 and CmHKT2 in CmHSFA4 were much higher than the WT with or without salinity

23.q: Under salinity stress, ROS scavenger genes and CmHSPs were up‐regulated in both WT and OX plants, and the expression levels in OX plants were always higher than those in WT plants

24.q: Under salinity stress, ROS scavenger genes and CmHSPs were up‐regulated in both WT and OX plants, and the expression levels in OX plants were always higher than those in WT plants

25.q: Under salinity stress, ROS scavenger genes and CmHSPs were up‐regulated in both WT and OX plants, and the expression levels in OX plants were always higher than those in WT plants

26.q: Under salinity stress, ROS scavenger genes and CmHSPs were up-regulated in both WT and OX plants, and the expression levels in OX plants were always higher than those in WT plants (Figure 9d–h).

27.q: Under salinity stress, ROS scavenger genes and CmHSPs were up-regulated in both WT and OX plants, and the expression levels in OX plants were always higher than those in WT plants (Figure 9d–h).

28.q: CmHSFA4 directly binds to the promoter of CmMYB121 (Fig. 4B). To further examine whether CmHSFA4 could specifically bind to the HSE cis-elements of CmMYB121 promoters in vitro, 42-bp promoter fragments containing HSE or mutant HSE were biotin-labeled for electrophoretic mobility shift assays.

29.q: DgNHX1-overexpression plants accumulate more Na+ and K+, correlated with an increase activity of the vacuolar Na+/H+ antiporter, which plays important roles in the compartmentation of Na+ and K+ highly accumulated in the cytoplasm into the vacuoles.

30.q: Expression of AtSOS1, AtSOS2, AtSOS3 was reduced in the CmWRKY17 transgenic Arabidopsis compared with that in the wild-type Col-0.

31.q: the isolation of a chrysanthemum cDNA belonging to the HSP70 family is reported... particularly associated with acquired thermotolerance

32.q: The data demonstrated that Na+/H+ antiporter activity of four SOS1s was essential and AjSOS1, CrcSOS1 and CcSOS1 were fully able to exclude Na+ when expressed in yeast.

33.q: stress-responsive genes in transgenic chrysanthemum were greater up-regulated than in WT under salinity stress... qRT-PCR of DgCu/ZnSOD showed higher expression in transgenic lines.

34.q: stress-responsive genes in transgenic chrysanthemum were greater up-regulated than in WT under salinity stress... qRT-PCR of DgCu/ZnSOD showed higher expression in transgenic lines.
